key_insight: Neural EBSD replaces discrete-grid Kikuchi pattern storage with a continuous, differentiable neural field that can reconstruct and query electron backscatter diffraction data off-grid.
engineering_relevance: This enables super-resolution EBSD analysis, smoother boundary/defect localization, and major data compression for large microstructure datasets while retaining full-pattern access.
method: Represent the EBSD scan as a continuous 4D intensity field over specimen and detector coordinates.
Compare a joint coordinate-to-intensity network with a factorized model using specimen-domain coefficient fields and detector-domain basis patterns.
Use analytical spatial derivatives of the learned field for continuous boundary and heterogeneity mapping.
evidence_signal: On recrystallized and additively manufactured Inconel 718, the factorized model achieved map-averaged reconstruction errors below 1% of maximum intensity and enabled about 700-fold compression.
watch_item: Performance and fidelity depend on the quality of neural reconstruction, so extrapolation beyond the measured scan region or to strongly atypical patterns may require careful validation.
